If biological death occurs before upload is possible, cryonic preservation extends your options. Sign up for cryonics NOW as a bridge strategy while technology matures. The window for arrangement is open today.

🔭 BRAIN IMAGING COMPARISON 6 modalities compared: resolution, cost, and WBE suitability ×
⚠ What WBE Requires
Whole Brain Emulation requires nanometer-resolution 3D imaging of every synapse. Only electron microscopy achieves this today, but it requires physical sectioning (destructive). Future: non-destructive X-ray nanotomography or advanced optical methods may enable in-vivo scanning.

😴 SLEEP OPTIMIZER Sleep diary + sleep debt + evidence-based optimization guide ×
SLEEP DIARY — LAST 7 NIGHTS
EVIDENCE-BASED OPTIMIZATION GUIDE
⏰
Consistent schedule: Same wake time 7 days/week. This single habit anchors your circadian rhythm and is the highest-leverage sleep intervention available.
🌞
Temperature 65-68°F (18-20°C): Core body temperature must drop ~2°F to initiate sleep onset. A cool bedroom is non-negotiable for deep sleep quality.
🌜
Complete darkness: Even small light exposure suppresses melatonin by up to 50%. Use blackout curtains or a sleep mask. Especially important after 9PM.
📱
No screens 1 hr before bed: Blue light (450-490nm) directly suppresses melatonin and delays sleep onset by 1-2 hours. Night Shift mode is insufficient.
☕
Caffeine cutoff 2PM: Caffeine has a 5-7 hour half-life. A 3PM coffee at 200mg leaves 100mg in your system at 9PM — halving slow-wave sleep even if you fall asleep normally.
SLEEP STAGES & FUNCTIONS
N1 (5%): Transition
Hypnagogic state. 1-7 minutes. Hypnic jerks common. Easily awakened. Brain enters theta waves.
N2 (45%): Light Sleep
Sleep spindles — bursts of 12-15Hz activity that block external stimuli and consolidate procedural/motor memory. Heart rate and temperature drop.
N3 (25%): Deep Slow-Wave Sleep
CRITICAL: Glymphatic system activates — clears amyloid-beta, tau proteins, and metabolic waste from brain at 10x waking rate. Growth hormone secretion. Immune system repair. Most restorative stage.
REM (25%): Dream Sleep
Episodic memory consolidation — hippocampus replays memories to prefrontal cortex for long-term storage. Emotional memory processing. Creativity and insight synthesis. CRITICAL for learning. Digital minds don't require sleep — biological brains categorically do.
💡 Upload relevance: The glymphatic system's clearance of amyloid and tau during N3 sleep is directly relevant to Alzheimer's risk and brain preservation quality. Poor sleep for years literally deposits the proteins that make brains harder to scan and upload.
🎮 COGNITIVE TRAINING SUITE 4 mini-games: N-Back · Reaction · Pattern · Stroop · Composite score ×
😤 STRESS TRACKER Daily cortisol tracker + hippocampal damage mechanisms + interventions ×
TODAY'S STRESS LEVEL (1 = completely calm, 10 = crisis)
STRESS HEATMAP — LAST 30 DAYS (yellow=low, red=high)
HOW CHRONIC STRESS DAMAGES YOUR BRAIN
Cortisol + Hippocampus: Sustained high cortisol kills hippocampal neurons and suppresses neurogenesis. Chronic stress measurably shrinks the hippocampus — the memory center critical for upload viability.

Amygdala Hypertrophy: Threat-center grows larger under chronic stress. Impairs rational decision-making and creates anxiety feedback loops.

Prefrontal Weakening: Stress weakens PFC-amygdala regulation pathways — reducing emotional control and executive function.

Upload relevance: Each month of high chronic stress accelerates cellular aging (telomere shortening ~3x faster), reduces neuroplasticity, and degrades the neural architecture that must be captured for uploading.
EVIDENCE-BASED INTERVENTIONS
A
Aerobic exercise (30+ min/day) — Reduces cortisol baseline 15-20%. Grows hippocampus 2%/year. Most replicated stress-reduction intervention in neuroscience literature.
A
MBSR (8-week mindfulness program) — Reduces amygdala gray matter density and cortisol levels by up to 25%. Jon Kabat-Zinn's protocol: 10-20 min daily. Meta-analyses: consistent effect size 0.5-0.8.
B
Social bonding and oxytocin — Oxytocin directly downregulates cortisol and HPA axis activity. Regular quality time with close relationships provides measurable cortisol buffering.
B
Sleep optimization — HPA axis resets during deep sleep. Chronic sleep deprivation elevates cortisol by 20-40%. Fixing sleep is often the most impactful first step in stress reduction.
C
CBT / therapy for chronic stressors — Cognitive restructuring changes the cortisol response to specific stressors. Particularly effective for workplace-related and relationship-induced chronic stress.
C
Adaptogens (Ashwagandha, Rhodiola) — Some human trial data for cortisol reduction. Ashwagandha: 300-600mg/day. Effect moderate but consistent in RCTs. Use as adjunct, not primary intervention.
🥗 BRAIN NUTRITION 6 key brain nutrients + avoid list + 3-day meal plan ×
KEY BRAIN NUTRIENTS
Omega-3 (DHA + EPA)2-3g DHA/EPA daily
Mechanism: DHA is a structural membrane component of neuronal synapses (40% of brain fatty acids). EPA reduces neuroinflammation via prostaglandin modulation. Low omega-3 index predicts smaller brain volume and 40% higher dementia risk.
🍟 Fatty fish 3x/week (salmon, sardines, mackerel) • Algae-based DHA (preferred for vegans) • OmegaQuant index test: target 8-12%
Choline550mg/day (men) · 425mg/day (women)
Mechanism: Precursor to acetylcholine — critical neurotransmitter for memory encoding and hippocampal function. Essential for myelin sheath synthesis. 90% of Americans deficient.
🦔 Eggs (1 large = 125mg) • Beef liver (highest source) • Salmon • Supplements: Alpha-GPC (best bioavailability) or CDP-choline
Magnesium-L-Threonate144mg elemental Mg (Mg-L-T form)
Mechanism: Mg-L-Threonate uniquely crosses the blood-brain barrier to raise brain Mg. Blocks NMDA receptors at rest (prevents excitotoxicity), increases synaptic density. MIT research: improves learning and memory in aging rodents.
🌿 Dark leafy greens, pumpkin seeds, dark chocolate • Supplements: Mg-L-Threonate (cognition) or Mg glycinate (sleep/relaxation)
Vitamin B122.4mcg/day (higher for 50+)
Mechanism: Essential for myelin synthesis, DNA methylation, and homocysteine regulation. Deficiency causes progressive demyelination of neurons, cognitive decline, and depression. Absorption declines with age.
🥩 Meat, fish, shellfish, dairy • Fortified nutritional yeast/plant milk • Supplement: methylcobalamin 1000mcg/day (preferred over cyanocobalamin)
Zinc8-11mg elemental zinc/day
Mechanism: Modulates NMDA and AMPA synaptic transmission. Required for adult neurogenesis. High concentration in hippocampus and amygdala. Deficiency impairs memory formation and emotional regulation.
🍲 Oysters (richest source) • Beef, lamb • Pumpkin seeds, hemp seeds • Supplement: zinc picolinate or bisglycinate (best absorbed)
Flavonoids (anthocyanins, EGCG, quercetin)500-1000mg via food
Mechanism: Potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant. Increase cerebral blood flow, boost BDNF, reduce amyloid aggregation. MIND diet (berry-rich variant) reduces Alzheimer's risk 35% in prospective studies.
🍇 Blueberries (daily) • Dark chocolate 70%+ (daily) • Green tea (3 cups) • Pomegranate juice • Red/purple berries
🌟 The Digital Advantage: Digital minds require no nutrition whatsoever. But biological brains — and the upload process itself — depend critically on the neuronal integrity maintained through decades of optimal nutrition. Every deficiency today shapes the brain that will eventually be scanned.
WHAT TO AVOID
✗Trans fats — directly impair hippocampal memory function. 75% increased dementia risk in high consumers. Found in fried/processed foods, margarine, packaged pastries.
✗Excess alcohol (>7-14 units/week) — direct neurotoxin. Shrinks hippocampus, depletes thiamine, damages white matter. Low-moderate wine: debated (flavonoids vs toxicity).
✗Ultra-processed foods — BMJ 2022 study (10,775 subjects): every 10% UPF increase associated with 28% faster cognitive decline. Mechanism: chronic inflammation, microbiome disruption.
✗Chronic high sugar / glycemic spikes — Alzheimer's called "Type 3 diabetes." Insulin resistance impairs BDNF, promotes tau phosphorylation and amyloid-beta accumulation.
3-DAY BRAIN-OPTIMIZED MEAL PLAN
DAY 1 — Omega-3 Focus
Breakfast: 2 eggs scrambled + smoked salmon + blueberries + green tea + Alpha-GPC 300mg
Lunch: Sardine salad with olive oil + walnuts + dark leafy greens + pomegranate seeds
Dinner: Grilled salmon (300g) + quinoa + broccoli + dark chocolate 25g (70%+)
Supplements: Omega-3 2g, Mg-L-Threonate 144mg, Methylcobalamin 1000mcg
DAY 2 — Anti-inflammatory Focus
Breakfast: Oatmeal + mixed berries (200g) + walnuts + turmeric latte with black pepper
Lunch: Chicken + lentil soup + turmeric + ginger + leafy green salad
Dinner: Grass-fed beef liver (1x/month) or lean beef + sweet potato + steamed greens
Snacks: Pumpkin seeds (zinc), oysters if available, green tea x3
DAY 3 — Mediterranean Style
Breakfast: Avocado toast + 2 poached eggs + tomato + extra virgin olive oil drizzle
Lunch: Tuna (skipjack, low mercury) + brown rice + miso soup + seaweed salad
Dinner: Mackerel or herring + roasted Mediterranean vegetables + 1 glass red wine (optional)
Supplements: Zinc picolinate 11mg, Vitamin D3 2000IU, B-complex + methylfolate
🏃 EXERCISE & NEUROGENESIS BDNF, hippocampal growth, and your personal exercise log ×
BDNF (Brain-Derived Neurotrophic Factor) is the molecular basis of neuroplasticity. It enables synaptic strengthening, neuron survival, and is the primary trigger for adult hippocampal neurogenesis. Exercise is the single most powerful known stimulator of BDNF — more effective than any supplement, food, or drug.
BDNF STIMULATION BY EXERCISE TYPE
HIIT (85-95% HRmax)
+100%
Aerobic (Zone 2)
+75%
Resistance Training
+55%
Yoga / Flexibility
+25%
● Adult neurogenesis (new neurons in hippocampus) requires exercise + novel environments + sleep. Without all three, newly-generated neurons don't survive more than a few weeks.
● Aerobic exercise grows hippocampal volume by ~2% per year (vs 1-2% annual shrinkage without exercise). Every 10% VO2max improvement predicts ~1 year younger biological age.

RESULT
Digital Sovereignty

Your mind should be as private as your thoughts. In a world where digital consciousness is possible, the question of who can access, copy, or modify your digital self becomes existential — not merely a privacy concern.

Real encryption for digital minds requires:
● AES-256-GCM for data at rest
● Post-quantum algorithms (CRYSTALS-Kyber, CRYSTALS-Dilithium) as quantum computers emerge
● Zero-knowledge proofs for identity verification without revealing contents
● Distributed key management — no single authority holds your keys

📊 SCIENTIFIC CONSENSUS Where does science stand on 10 key questions? ×
📝 2024 PROGRESS REPORT Annual state-of-the-field: digital immortality research ×
Executive Summary
2024 marks a turning point. Three developments — Neuralink's first human implant, the Google/Harvard cortex mapping milestone, and continued AI progress — have moved whole brain emulation from theoretical to engineering challenge. The field is no longer asking if but when.
564
in cryosuspension
38
BCI patients
57K
neurons mapped (1mm³)
~2040
mouse WBE estimate
01BRAIN MAPPING (CONNECTOMICS)
The landmark Google/Harvard collaboration published the largest human cortex map to date: 1mm³ containing 57,000 neurons and 150 million synapses, producing 1.4 petabytes of data. The complete adult Drosophila connectome (130,000 neurons) was published in 2023.
💡 KEY INSIGHT
At current rates, a complete human connectome would require ~1.5 million years of equivalent effort. AI-accelerated segmentation is reducing this by 3–4 orders of magnitude.
References: Shapson-Coe et al., Science 2024; Dorkenwald et al., Nature 2023
02BCI TECHNOLOGY
Neuralink's N1 implant: 1024 electrodes, wireless, enabling cursor control and typing at 40 WPM by thought alone. Synchron's Stentrode provides endovascular BCI access. Bandwidth remains critical: current BCIs record ~1000 neurons vs. 86 billion total.
💡 KEY INSIGHT
Current BCI bandwidth: ~1 Kbps useful information. Estimated minimum for full upload: ~100 Tbps. Gap: 11 orders of magnitude. This is the single largest technical challenge.
03CONSCIOUSNESS RESEARCH
Three major theories: IIT (Tononi), GWT (Dehaene), and Predictive Processing (Friston). A landmark 2023 adversarial collaboration tested IIT vs GWT — results were inconclusive, demonstrating the profound difficulty of empirical consciousness research.
04BRAIN PRESERVATION
Aldehyde-Stabilized Cryopreservation (ASC) won the Brain Preservation Prize in 2016 for preserving nanoscale neural structure in large mammalian brains. The key debate: is structural preservation sufficient, or do we also need to preserve dynamic patterns?
05COMPUTE PROGRESS
Simulating the human brain in real time requires ~10¹⁸ FLOPS. The Frontier supercomputer (2022) achieves exactly this — but at $600M cost. Neuromorphic chips offer 100-1000x efficiency gains. At current improvement rates, a desktop computer will have sufficient FLOPS by ~2045.
💡 KEY INSIGHT
At current improvement rates, a desktop computer will have sufficient FLOPS for human brain simulation by approximately 2045 — matching Kurzweil's original Singularity prediction.
06KEY CHALLENGES
Four fundamental challenges: (1) The Hard Problem — we cannot verify if a digital mind is conscious; (2) Scan Resolution — nanoscale scanning destroys living tissue; (3) Simulation Fidelity — how detailed must it be?; (4) Identity Verification — is the simulation truly continuous with the original?
07OUTLOOK 2025–2030
Near-term milestones: (2025) Neuralink N2 with 10K+ electrodes; (2026) Complete mouse connectome; (2027) Real-time mouse cortex simulation; (2028) First whole mouse brain emulation attempt; (2029) Human-level AI (per Kurzweil); (2030) BCI bandwidth reaches megabit range.
💡 KEY INSIGHT
The probability of first human WBE by 2050 has increased from ~15% (2015 estimate) to ~35% (2024 estimate), driven by AI acceleration of neuroscience research.

😴 SLEEP OPTIMIZER Sleep diary + sleep debt + evidence-based optimization guide ×
SLEEP DIARY — LAST 7 NIGHTS
EVIDENCE-BASED OPTIMIZATION GUIDE
⏰
Consistent schedule: Same wake time 7 days/week. This single habit anchors your circadian rhythm and is the highest-leverage sleep intervention available.
🌞
Temperature 65-68°F (18-20°C): Core body temperature must drop ~2°F to initiate sleep onset. A cool bedroom is non-negotiable for deep sleep quality.
🌜
Complete darkness: Even small light exposure suppresses melatonin by up to 50%. Use blackout curtains or a sleep mask. Especially important after 9PM.
📱
No screens 1 hr before bed: Blue light (450-490nm) directly suppresses melatonin and delays sleep onset by 1-2 hours. Night Shift mode is insufficient.
☕
Caffeine cutoff 2PM: Caffeine has a 5-7 hour half-life. A 3PM coffee at 200mg leaves 100mg in your system at 9PM — halving slow-wave sleep even if you fall asleep normally.
SLEEP STAGES & FUNCTIONS
N1 (5%): Transition
Hypnagogic state. 1-7 minutes. Hypnic jerks common. Easily awakened. Brain enters theta waves.
N2 (45%): Light Sleep
Sleep spindles — bursts of 12-15Hz activity that block external stimuli and consolidate procedural/motor memory. Heart rate and temperature drop.
N3 (25%): Deep Slow-Wave Sleep
CRITICAL: Glymphatic system activates — clears amyloid-beta, tau proteins, and metabolic waste from brain at 10x waking rate. Growth hormone secretion. Immune system repair. Most restorative stage.
REM (25%): Dream Sleep
Episodic memory consolidation — hippocampus replays memories to prefrontal cortex for long-term storage. Emotional memory processing. Creativity and insight synthesis. CRITICAL for learning. Digital minds don't require sleep — biological brains categorically do.
💡 Upload relevance: The glymphatic system's clearance of amyloid and tau during N3 sleep is directly relevant to Alzheimer's risk and brain preservation quality. Poor sleep for years literally deposits the proteins that make brains harder to scan and upload.
🎮 COGNITIVE TRAINING SUITE 4 mini-games: N-Back · Reaction · Pattern · Stroop · Composite score ×
😤 STRESS TRACKER Daily cortisol tracker + hippocampal damage mechanisms + interventions ×
TODAY'S STRESS LEVEL (1 = completely calm, 10 = crisis)
STRESS HEATMAP — LAST 30 DAYS (yellow=low, red=high)
HOW CHRONIC STRESS DAMAGES YOUR BRAIN
Cortisol + Hippocampus: Sustained high cortisol kills hippocampal neurons and suppresses neurogenesis. Chronic stress measurably shrinks the hippocampus — the memory center critical for upload viability.

Amygdala Hypertrophy: Threat-center grows larger under chronic stress. Impairs rational decision-making and creates anxiety feedback loops.

Prefrontal Weakening: Stress weakens PFC-amygdala regulation pathways — reducing emotional control and executive function.

Upload relevance: Each month of high chronic stress accelerates cellular aging (telomere shortening ~3x faster), reduces neuroplasticity, and degrades the neural architecture that must be captured for uploading.
EVIDENCE-BASED INTERVENTIONS
A
Aerobic exercise (30+ min/day) — Reduces cortisol baseline 15-20%. Grows hippocampus 2%/year. Most replicated stress-reduction intervention in neuroscience literature.
A
MBSR (8-week mindfulness program) — Reduces amygdala gray matter density and cortisol levels by up to 25%. Jon Kabat-Zinn's protocol: 10-20 min daily. Meta-analyses: consistent effect size 0.5-0.8.
B
Social bonding and oxytocin — Oxytocin directly downregulates cortisol and HPA axis activity. Regular quality time with close relationships provides measurable cortisol buffering.
B
Sleep optimization — HPA axis resets during deep sleep. Chronic sleep deprivation elevates cortisol by 20-40%. Fixing sleep is often the most impactful first step in stress reduction.
C
CBT / therapy for chronic stressors — Cognitive restructuring changes the cortisol response to specific stressors. Particularly effective for workplace-related and relationship-induced chronic stress.
C
Adaptogens (Ashwagandha, Rhodiola) — Some human trial data for cortisol reduction. Ashwagandha: 300-600mg/day. Effect moderate but consistent in RCTs. Use as adjunct, not primary intervention.
🥗 BRAIN NUTRITION 6 key brain nutrients + avoid list + 3-day meal plan ×
KEY BRAIN NUTRIENTS
Omega-3 (DHA + EPA)2-3g DHA/EPA daily
Mechanism: DHA is a structural membrane component of neuronal synapses (40% of brain fatty acids). EPA reduces neuroinflammation via prostaglandin modulation. Low omega-3 index predicts smaller brain volume and 40% higher dementia risk.
🍟 Fatty fish 3x/week (salmon, sardines, mackerel) • Algae-based DHA (preferred for vegans) • OmegaQuant index test: target 8-12%
Choline550mg/day (men) · 425mg/day (women)
Mechanism: Precursor to acetylcholine — critical neurotransmitter for memory encoding and hippocampal function. Essential for myelin sheath synthesis. 90% of Americans deficient.
🦔 Eggs (1 large = 125mg) • Beef liver (highest source) • Salmon • Supplements: Alpha-GPC (best bioavailability) or CDP-choline
Magnesium-L-Threonate144mg elemental Mg (Mg-L-T form)
Mechanism: Mg-L-Threonate uniquely crosses the blood-brain barrier to raise brain Mg. Blocks NMDA receptors at rest (prevents excitotoxicity), increases synaptic density. MIT research: improves learning and memory in aging rodents.
🌿 Dark leafy greens, pumpkin seeds, dark chocolate • Supplements: Mg-L-Threonate (cognition) or Mg glycinate (sleep/relaxation)
Vitamin B122.4mcg/day (higher for 50+)
Mechanism: Essential for myelin synthesis, DNA methylation, and homocysteine regulation. Deficiency causes progressive demyelination of neurons, cognitive decline, and depression. Absorption declines with age.
🥩 Meat, fish, shellfish, dairy • Fortified nutritional yeast/plant milk • Supplement: methylcobalamin 1000mcg/day (preferred over cyanocobalamin)
Zinc8-11mg elemental zinc/day
Mechanism: Modulates NMDA and AMPA synaptic transmission. Required for adult neurogenesis. High concentration in hippocampus and amygdala. Deficiency impairs memory formation and emotional regulation.
🍲 Oysters (richest source) • Beef, lamb • Pumpkin seeds, hemp seeds • Supplement: zinc picolinate or bisglycinate (best absorbed)
Flavonoids (anthocyanins, EGCG, quercetin)500-1000mg via food
Mechanism: Potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant. Increase cerebral blood flow, boost BDNF, reduce amyloid aggregation. MIND diet (berry-rich variant) reduces Alzheimer's risk 35% in prospective studies.
🍇 Blueberries (daily) • Dark chocolate 70%+ (daily) • Green tea (3 cups) • Pomegranate juice • Red/purple berries
🌟 The Digital Advantage: Digital minds require no nutrition whatsoever. But biological brains — and the upload process itself — depend critically on the neuronal integrity maintained through decades of optimal nutrition. Every deficiency today shapes the brain that will eventually be scanned.
WHAT TO AVOID
✗Trans fats — directly impair hippocampal memory function. 75% increased dementia risk in high consumers. Found in fried/processed foods, margarine, packaged pastries.
✗Excess alcohol (>7-14 units/week) — direct neurotoxin. Shrinks hippocampus, depletes thiamine, damages white matter. Low-moderate wine: debated (flavonoids vs toxicity).
✗Ultra-processed foods — BMJ 2022 study (10,775 subjects): every 10% UPF increase associated with 28% faster cognitive decline. Mechanism: chronic inflammation, microbiome disruption.
✗Chronic high sugar / glycemic spikes — Alzheimer's called "Type 3 diabetes." Insulin resistance impairs BDNF, promotes tau phosphorylation and amyloid-beta accumulation.
3-DAY BRAIN-OPTIMIZED MEAL PLAN
DAY 1 — Omega-3 Focus
Breakfast: 2 eggs scrambled + smoked salmon + blueberries + green tea + Alpha-GPC 300mg
Lunch: Sardine salad with olive oil + walnuts + dark leafy greens + pomegranate seeds
Dinner: Grilled salmon (300g) + quinoa + broccoli + dark chocolate 25g (70%+)
Supplements: Omega-3 2g, Mg-L-Threonate 144mg, Methylcobalamin 1000mcg
DAY 2 — Anti-inflammatory Focus
Breakfast: Oatmeal + mixed berries (200g) + walnuts + turmeric latte with black pepper
Lunch: Chicken + lentil soup + turmeric + ginger + leafy green salad
Dinner: Grass-fed beef liver (1x/month) or lean beef + sweet potato + steamed greens
Snacks: Pumpkin seeds (zinc), oysters if available, green tea x3
DAY 3 — Mediterranean Style
Breakfast: Avocado toast + 2 poached eggs + tomato + extra virgin olive oil drizzle
Lunch: Tuna (skipjack, low mercury) + brown rice + miso soup + seaweed salad
Dinner: Mackerel or herring + roasted Mediterranean vegetables + 1 glass red wine (optional)
Supplements: Zinc picolinate 11mg, Vitamin D3 2000IU, B-complex + methylfolate
🏃 EXERCISE & NEUROGENESIS BDNF, hippocampal growth, and your personal exercise log ×
BDNF (Brain-Derived Neurotrophic Factor) is the molecular basis of neuroplasticity. It enables synaptic strengthening, neuron survival, and is the primary trigger for adult hippocampal neurogenesis. Exercise is the single most powerful known stimulator of BDNF — more effective than any supplement, food, or drug.
BDNF STIMULATION BY EXERCISE TYPE
HIIT (85-95% HRmax)
+100%
Aerobic (Zone 2)
+75%
Resistance Training
+55%
Yoga / Flexibility
+25%
● Adult neurogenesis (new neurons in hippocampus) requires exercise + novel environments + sleep. Without all three, newly-generated neurons don't survive more than a few weeks.
● Aerobic exercise grows hippocampal volume by ~2% per year (vs 1-2% annual shrinkage without exercise). Every 10% VO2max improvement predicts ~1 year younger biological age.
LOG EXERCISE SESSION

💡 Upload relevance: Exercise directly improves the quality of the brain being archived — more synaptic density, more hippocampal volume, less amyloid deposition. An exercised brain is a better-preserved, more complete brain to upload.

Digital Sovereignty

Your mind should be as private as your thoughts. In a world where digital consciousness is possible, the question of who can access, copy, or modify your digital self becomes existential — not merely a privacy concern.

Real encryption for digital minds requires:
● AES-256-GCM for data at rest
● Post-quantum algorithms (CRYSTALS-Kyber, CRYSTALS-Dilithium) as quantum computers emerge
● Zero-knowledge proofs for identity verification without revealing contents
● Distributed key management — no single authority holds your keys
